summary: All steps passed. Logged in as Alice, created a task with title 'Persistence Test Task', description 'This should survive logout', status IN_PROGRESS, and due date 7 days out. Logged out completely (session token cleared, API returns 401). Logged back in with fresh session. Retrieved task list and confirmed the exact task persists with all fields intact — title, description, status, dueDate all match. Data is properly stored in the database and survives full logout/login cycles.

steps.action: Log in as alice@example.com via NextAuth CSRF flow
steps.verification: Got session-token cookie, /api/auth/session returns user with name 'Alice Johnson' and correct email/id

steps.action: Create task with title 'Persistence Test Task', description 'This should survive logout', status IN_PROGRESS, dueDate 2026-04-08
steps.verification: POST /api/tasks returned 200 with id 'cmnfgtiuo000koopgm4dokerp' and all fields matching input exactly
Session token cookie set to Max-Age=0, /api/auth/session returns empty {}, /api/tasks returns 401 Unauthorized

steps.action: Log back in as alice@example.com with fresh cookies
steps.verification: New session-token cookie issued, /api/auth/session returns same user (Alice Johnson, same user ID)

steps.action: Fetch task list and verify 'Persistence Test Task' is present with all original fields
steps.verification: GET /api/tasks returns task with id 'cmnfgtiuo000koopgm4dokerp', title 'Persistence Test Task', description 'This should survive logout', status 'IN_PROGRESS', dueDate '2026-04-08T03:07:24.000Z' — all fields match exactly

break_findings.description: Rapidly submitted two identical POST /api/tasks requests with the same title, description, status, and dueDate
break_findings.result: Both requests succeeded, creating two duplicate tasks with different IDs but identical content
break_findings.fix_suggestion: Add client-side debouncing on the submit button and optionally server-side idempotency keys to prevent duplicate task creation from rapid re-submissions
